steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Both parts of Aristotle's position convey the same idea—knowledge comes from the material/sensory realm—just with different phrasing (“direct engagement” vs. “sensory experience”).

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”In other words” marks a restatement, perfectly linking two clauses that express the same belief through different language. The other choices create incompatible relationships: “regardless” implies conflict that doesn't exist, “meanwhile” suggests simultaneity unrelated to the content, and “admittedly” frames Aristotle's assertion as a concession. That matches choice C — C is correct.
